name: blockade-recon
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``blockade-recon`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: cargo install --git https://github.com/AidoP/blockade-recon ; then: blockade-recon -i  (or -im after `airmon-ng start <iface>`)

examples.command: cargo install --git https://github.com/AidoP/blockade-recon ; then: blockade-recon -i  (or -im after `airmon-ng start <iface>`)

dependencies: crates: pcap, radiotap, oui (git: AidoP/rs-oui), eui48, tui, termion, clap
algorithms: radiotap header parsing for captured 802.11 frames
beacon IE/tag parsing (SSID, supported rates, country, vendor-specific tags)
data-frame address resolution (To DS / From DS) to infer client<->AP associations
MAC OUI vendor lookup for per-manufacturer device tallying
libpcap monitor-mode live capture with TUI session display/saving
produces: parsed beacon records: SSID, supported rates, country, vendor tags, timestamp
data-frame addressing: receiver/transmitter/destination/source/BSSID/seq-ctrl for client-AP association inference
saved capture sessions for later review
novelty: Not an airodump-ng wrapper — it is a from-scratch independent Rust implementation of radiotap + 802.11 frame parsing with an interactive TUI and session save. However, functionally it is a strict subset of airodump-ng: it parses only beacons, ACKs, and data frames; there is NO probe-request/probe-response sniffing, NO EAPOL/802.1X or handshake capture, NO deauth or any active/injection logic, NO channel-hopping plan, and OUI is used only for vendor tallying (no enrichment pipeline). The README's "spatial mapping with multiple devices" and "802.11 attack demonstrations" are unfinished aspirational goals with no implementation. Recon value is redundant versus airodump-ng; the only genuine differentiator is a native-Rust TUI sniffer experience.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
